channel.title: Assembly on Research Notes
channel.description: Recent content in Assembly on Research Notes

item.title: Analyzing Compiled Code
item.description: Visualizing simple functions compiled with GCC 15.2 into x86-64 assembly to analyze stack frame layouts, register usage, argument passing, and control-flow decisions. Assignment to Local Variables void func() { int x = 3; } func(): push rbp mov rbp, rsp mov DWORD PTR [rbp-4], 3 nop pop rbp ret push rbp saves the current value of the base pointer (rbp) onto the stack. This preserves the caller’s frame pointer so it can be restored when the function returns.
